# NTCP2 crypto fixture corpus

These deterministic fixtures are for Plan 032 local validation only. They
contain synthetic fixed keys and bytes, never operational keys, peer
identities, addresses, or network captures.

`vectors.tsv` contains the primitive, transcript, AEAD, AES-CBC, and split-KDF
values. Its cryptographic values were independently generated with Python
`cryptography` 41.0.7 and hashlib/HMAC, using the pinned NTCP2 protocol name
and the RFC 7748 X25519 implementation. The AES-CBC values were generated by
the same independent harness. The Rust tests consume these values without
copying implementation code.

`storage-static-key.hex` is a deterministic encoding fixture produced by the
version-1 storage-format test generator for RNG seed 25. It is checked by the
storage round-trip and malformed-record tests.

The adjacent `manifest.tsv` records every file's SHA-256 and is checked by
`scripts/check-ntcp2-vectors.sh`. Fixture values are redistributable synthetic
test material under the repository's eventual project license.
